title: Kepler Invariant Discovery — a conserved quantity from noisy orbit samples
strategies: baseline: squared speed v^2 = x3^2 + x4^2, only partially conserved (couples energy to the non-conserved 1/r term) -> ~0.1
energy-like invariant 0.5*(x3^2+x4^2) - mu/sqrt(x1^2+x2^2) with the constant mu fitted by minimising within-orbit variance; recovers energy but the 1/r term amplifies noise -> medium
angular momentum L = x1*x4 - x2*x3, found by searching bilinear forms for the one most constant within orbits; no fitted constant, no noise-amplifying term -> highest
alternatives: Laplace-Runge-Lenz components, monotone transforms/combinations of E and L, or a joint fit trading complexity penalty against held-out conservation
